title: Pandemic Protocol
premise: A novel virus has emerged in a major city. Early data is incomplete and contradictory. You must decide whether to impose a lockdown, balancing public health, economic fallout, and political pressure, with the mayor's re-election campaign hanging in the balance.
setting: A large metropolis (population 5 million) in the present day. The city's health department headquarters, city hall, and media centers are the primary locations. The virus has a 2% case fatality rate based on initial reports but may be more or less deadly.
goal: Decide on a containment strategy (lockdown, partial measures, or no lockdown) within 3 days. Success: minimize deaths and economic damage while maintaining public trust. Failure: outbreak spirals, economy crashes, or political backlash ends the mayor's career.
gm_notes: Introduce conflicting data: a leaked report suggests the virus is mild; another shows it's airborne. Have the epidemiologist push for lockdown, the economist warn of recession, and the mayor's chief of staff hint at re-election concerns. Add a whistleblower from the lab claiming data manipulation. Pace with daily briefings and escalating cases.

roles.name: City Health Commissioner
roles.description: You are the top public health official, responsible for medical advice and containment. You have a background in infectious disease.
roles.private_info: You have a personal friend who is a virologist at the CDC; they off the record say the virus is likely highly contagious. You also know that the city's hospital capacity is already 80% full from flu season.

roles.name: City Economic Development Director
roles.description: You are tasked with minimizing economic disruption. The city's budget is tight, and a lockdown could bankrupt small businesses.
roles.private_info: You have been approached by a major downtown employer threatening to move headquarters if a lockdown is imposed. Also, the mayor's campaign donor list includes many business owners who would suffer.

roles.name: Mayor's Chief of Staff
roles.description: You are the mayor's right hand, focused on political optics and re-election. The mayor is up for re-election in 6 months.
roles.private_info: The mayor is trailing in polls. A lockdown could be blamed for economic pain, but no action could be seen as negligence. You have a secret polling memo showing that voters are evenly split but swing voters prioritize the economy. You also know the mayor's opponent has already called for a lockdown.

npcs.name: Dr. Elena Vasquez
npcs.description: City epidemiologist, lead on outbreak analysis. She is calm, data-driven, and respected.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She has a family member in the city's largest hospital and fears for their safety. She will push aggressively for lockdown even if data is ambiguous, perhaps exaggerating risks.

npcs.name: Marcus Webb
npcs.description: CEO of the city's largest employer (a tech firm). He is influential and connected.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He is planning a major expansion and a lockdown would delay it. He has offered a campaign donation to the mayor in exchange for no lockdown. If lockdown happens, he will move jobs to another state.

npcs.name: Reporter Jenna Kwan
npcs.description: Investigative journalist for the city's main newspaper. She is ambitious and has sources in the health department.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She has obtained leaked documents suggesting the virus may have been accidentally released from a lab. She is trying to verify before publishing, but may go public to force transparency.
